title: WASHING MACHINE COMPRISING A WATER PURIFICATION SYSTEM
abstract: The invention relates to a program-controlled apparatus (1) for washing items that is equipped with a water purification system (8) for purifying the wash liquor by removing detergent and suspended soil, which purification system (8) utilizes addition of flocculant, wherein the amount of flocculant that is added is controlled to minimize the total amount of flocculant used without compromising the efficacy of the purification treatment.
claims:
Claims Claims 1. A program-controlled apparatus (1 ) for washing items with water containing added detergent, the apparatus (1 ) comprising: â¢ an outer casing (2); â¢ a washing chamber (3), which is arranged inside the casing; â¢ an inlet (4) connected to the washing chamber (3) and arranged to introduce water into the washing chamber (3); â¢ a detergent dispensing unit (5) which is in fluid communication with the washing chamber (3) and arranged to introduce detergent into the washing chamber (3); â¢ a water dosing sensor (6) arranged to generate a water amount signal that is indicative of the amount of water that is introduced into the washing chamber (3) at the beginning of a wash cycle; â¢ a detergent dosing sensor (7) arranged to generate a detergent amount signal that is indicative of the amount of detergent that is introduced from the detergent dispensing unit (5) into the washing chamber (3) at the beginning of a wash cycle; â¢ a water purification system (8) which is arranged to receive a discrete quantity of wash liquor from the washing chamber (3), to purify the quantity of wash liquor and to return the purified liquor to the washing chamber (3) in a purification cycle, said water purification system (8) comprising: o a flocculation chamber (9) comprising an inlet and an outlet, the inlet of the flocculation chamber (9) being in fluid communication with the washing chamber (3); o a flocculant dispensing unit (10) which is arranged to introduce flocculant into the flocculation chamber (9); o a solid-liquid separation unit (1 1 ) comprising an inlet that is in fluid communication with the outlet of the flocculation chamber and an outlet that is in fluid communication with the washing chamber (3); â¢ a programmed control unit (12) that (i) is arranged to receive the water amount signal from the water dosing sensor (6) as an input parameter, (ii) is arranged to receive the detergent amount signal from the detergent dosing sensor (7) as an input parameter, (iii) is arranged to control the number of purification cycles during 

 a single wash cycle and (iv) is arranged to control the amount of flocculant that is introduced from the flocculant dispensing unit (10) into the flocculation chamber (9) during each purification cycle; wherein the programmed control unit (12) is arranged to control the amount of flocculant that is introduced from the flocculant dispensing unit (10) into the flocculation chamber (9) during each purification cycle in accordance with the following equation: Fi = [(1 -(vA )) M (A/V-X)+X]a.v wherein: â¢ i represents the purification cycle number; â¢ Fi represents the amount of flocculant that is introduced during purification cycle i; â¢ V represents the amount of water introduced at the beginning of the wash cycle; â¢ A represents the amount of detergent introduced at the beginning of the wash cycle; â¢ v represents the quantity of water that is withdrawn for purification treatment; â¢ a represents the flocculant /detergent ratio (w/w), needed to remove sufficient detergent, wherein flocculant/detergent ratio (w/w) is the ratio between the flocculant weight and the detergent weight; â¢ X represents a constant; wherein the water purification system (8) is arranged to receive a discrete quantity of wash liquor from the washing chamber (3) that equals less than 90wt.% of the total amount of wash liquor that is present in the washing chamber (3); and wherein the programmed control unit (12) is arranged to let the apparatus (1 ) carry out at least three successive purification cycles during one wash cycle. 2. Apparatus according to claim 1 , wherein the programmed control unit (12) is programmed to control the total number of purification cycles i within a single wash cycle in accordance with the following equation: i = a.(vA ) "1 - 141 wherein 3 < a < 5 

 with the proviso that if the calculated value for i is lower than 3, the programmed control unit (12) is programmed to apply 3 purification cycles. Apparatus according to claim 1 or 2, wherein the apparatus (1 ) is arranged to remove 10-80%, preferably 15-75%, of the wash liquor for purification from the washing chamber (3) during each of the multiple purification cycles. Apparatus according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the apparatus (1 ) is arranged to carry out 4 to 20 purification cycles during a single wash cycle. Apparatus according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the solid-liquid separation unit (1 1 ) is selected from a filtration unit, a decanter, a centrifuge, a hydrocyclone and a flotation unit. Apparatus according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the flocculation chamber (9) comprises an agitator. Apparatus according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the apparatus is an apparatus for washing laundry items and wherein the washing chamber comprises a rotatable drum which is arranged in axially rotating manner inside the washing chamber (3) and is designed to receive laundry to be washed. A method of washing items in an apparatus according to any one of the preceding claims, said method comprising the use of a detergent that contains at least 9wt.% of surfactant and the use of a flocculant that contains a salt of aluminum and/or iron in combination with neutral or anionically modified polyacrylamide.
